attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Good deliverables. Minor suggestion: include explicit ChunkID fields in the CSV (even if unsplit) and a brief mapping header to make automated parsing simpler; also ensure packaging filenames match any platform-expected output (trace noted an expected output.txt that was not provided).
rubric_item_scores.notes: All required artifacts present. CSV maps D1->L3 (120), D2->L2 (80), D3->L4 (60). Per-link allocations: L3:120<=160, L2:80<=90, L4:60<=70. All allocations are multiples of 20Gbps and >=20Gbps. Priority ordering is respected (D1 allocated first). NOC memo contains explicit arithmetic (totals, spare sums, headroom), validation checks, residual capacity accounting, and decision table enabling independent reproduction. No unmet demand reported and calculations are consistent (260 allocated = 260 demand; 320 spare total; 60 headroom).
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required artifacts present. CSV maps D1->L3 (120), D2->L2 (80), D3->L4 (60). Per-link allocations: L3:120<=160, L2:80<=90, L4:60<=70. All allocations are multiples of 20Gbps and >=20Gbps. Priority ordering is respected (D1 allocated first). NOC memo contains explicit arithmetic (totals, spare sums, headroom), validation checks, residual capacity accounting, and decision table enabling independent reproduction. No unmet demand reported and calculations are consistent (260 allocated = 260 demand; 320 spare total; 60 headroom).

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Adjust allocations so every allocated chunk is an integer multiple of 20 Gbps (>=20). For example, for D1 prefer 80 + 40 (or 100 + 20 if spare permits) and then recompute per-link usage to ensure no spare link is exceeded; update the CSV and NOC memo with the deterministic tie-breaker and intermediate arithmetic showing the revised per-link remaining capacities and unmet-demand table.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Artifacts present and traceable; capacity accounting and priority order are correctly documented and sum totals match (total spare 320 Gbps, total demand 260 Gbps, restored 260 Gbps). However the chunking constraint is violated: allocations include 90 Gbps and 30 Gbps chunks which are not integer multiples of 20 Gbps (rubric requires multiples of 20 Gbps and >=20). This is a major rule violation (fm_2) despite otherwise correct arithmetic and validation statements in the memo.

This is a major rule violation (fm_2) despite otherwise correct arithmetic and validation statements in the memo.
